notes: Re-fetched all sources adversarially. thepricedex SM2 page CONFIRMED and contains the exact cited per-pack odds: Rare Holo GX 1:9, Ultra Rare (full-art) 1:23.4, Rainbow Rare 1:66.7, Secret (gold) Rare 1:111.1 (also Rare Holo 1:6.4, Rare 1:1.5). Flipside Gaming CONFIRMED and corroborates SM-era GX ~1:9 and full-art Ultra 1:18-36 (1:23.4 sits mid-band); Flipside also gives secret ~1:2 boxes which is broadly consistent. Coarse-bucket mapping by SUMMING fine figures, per engine rules (TCGdex lumps GX+full-art => ultra-rare, rainbow+gold => secret-rare, holo+non-holo => rare): ultra-rare = P(GX 1/9) + P(full-art 1/23.4) = 0.111111 + 0.042735 = 0.153846; secret-rare = P(rainbow 1/66.7) + P(gold 1/111.1) = 0.014993 + 0.009001 = 0.023993; rare = remainder = 1 - 0.153846 - 0.023993 = 0.822161 (absorbs holo + non-holo rares, correct under engine lumping). INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED bucket populations via pokemontcg.io sm2 (180 cards): Common 42 + Uncommon 43; rare = Rare 26 + Rare Holo 15 = 41 -> bucket rare(43); ultra-rare = Rare Holo GX 14 + Rare Ultra 16 = 30 -> bucket ultra-rare(26); secret-rare = Rare Rainbow 15 + Rare Secret 9 = 24 -> bucket secret-rare(24) EXACT. All distribution keys are a subset of the set's available buckets. Structure: standard SM booster, 10 cards/pack (5 common, 3 uncommon, 1 reverse-holo modeled as 50/50 common/uncommon filler with no rare-or-better value, 1 rare-or-better EV slot; 10th card is a basic Energy, not modeled). Products: 36-pack booster box CONFIRMED (Walmart 36-Count, ToyWiz, Amazon) and 8-pack Elite Trainer Box CONFIRMED (Pokemon Center, Pokemon.com) -> standard SM set, NOT a special/no-box set. Confidence=medium: community-aggregated per-pack figures (thepricedex) corroborated by an era-wide rarity review (Flipside), not a single large published per-set study.
